summary: McKenzie provides a brief progress update on the web version of Bingo Card Creator after spending a day working with a local designer. The redesign covers most screens except the main editing interface. He shares his remaining to-do list before launch, including password recovery, analytics, and purchase integration. The post is a short development log typical of his early blogging style.
key_points: Working with a designer improved UI and usability beyond what McKenzie could achieve alone
Prioritizing launch readiness over perfection — analytics can be postponed but purchasing integration cannot
Even a brief update post includes a natural SEO-optimized internal link to holiday bingo cards
money_quotes: He has a much better eye for design issues, including UI and usability issues, than I do.
It actually now looks almost pretty.
I would include screenshots except I am on Linux and GIMP scares me.
key_insight: Hiring a designer to complement your technical skills produces better results than trying to do everything yourself.
